title: Science All Around Us
description: Explore space, biology, chemistry, weather, and everyday physics.

questions.prompt: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.hint: It comes from the Latin word 'aurum.'

questions.prompt: Which organ pumps blood through the human body?
questions.explanation: The heart pumps blood through the circulatory system.

questions.prompt: The force that pulls objects toward Earth is _____.
questions.hint: It also keeps planets in orbit around the Sun.
questions.explanation: Gravity is the force of attraction between masses.

questions.prompt: Which part of a plant carries out most photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Leaves contain chlorophyll and are the main site of photosynthesis in many plants.

questions.prompt: Sound travels faster in water than in air.
questions.hint: Particles are closer together in water.
questions.explanation: Sound generally travels faster in water than in air because water transmits vibrations more efficiently.

questions.prompt: What is the largest planet in our solar system?
questions.hint: It is famous for the Great Red Spot.
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system.

questions.prompt: Which scale is commonly used to measure earthquake magnitude?
questions.hint: It is named after a seismologist.
questions.explanation: The Richter scale is a well-known scale for earthquake magnitude, though modern measurements often use related moment magnitude scales.

questions.prompt: Which blood cells help fight infection?
questions.hint: Their name points to their color category, not their job.
questions.explanation: White blood cells are part of the immune system and help fight infections.

questions.hint: It has two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.
questions.explanation: Water is made of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom, giving it the formula H2O.

questions.prompt: What type of celestial object is the Sun?
questions.hint: It produces light and heat through nuclear fusion.
questions.explanation: The Sun is a star at the center of our solar system.
